description: Sea lion carcass on the beach at Carmanah & no one was camping there when we passed by. Thought safer to not stick around that area
Bear walking past campsite, eating something on the beach. Sunset.

Going southbound the trail is not marked visibly after the river crossing, multiple paths lead to nowhere for a few hundred meters. The actual trail goes uphill to meet back up with the path from the broken bridge.
Cheewhat South Trail ladder is missing a single rung about 2/3 of the way up.
Bear hanging around camp, strolling north along the coast
Bear strolled beach by Klanawa camp heading south, swam across and vanished into the bushes.

Some genius took a dump and left it together with a mountain of toilet paper on the access trail to the reliable water source at Klanawa on the south side by the cable car. I would avoid this water. 

For now - water can be found between the cable car and beach access on south side. It looks like a murky pond with a lot of foam from the boardwalk but if you bushwack upstream 20 ft there is a nice clean trickle. I think this is seasonal so might not be there in summer months.
Bridge 66 looks like it's missing 2 of the 3 support beams, 1 of them likely will fail soon. no easy way around either

Walbran Ford is tricky this year. Winter storms moved a lot of the gravel out and it seems harder to ford to me. It used to be just a foot or so at low tide at the mouth but today was over knee deep at the mouth. I chose to ford closer to shore - but it’s tricky - flow is harder and you are crossing on variable height sandstone bed with some really deep bits if you miss.
KM71 Ladder missing a rung in the middle.
Km73 ladder with four rungs missing has been repaired by guardians. Only missing the very bottom rung which was 6” off the ground so not a problem anymore.
2 bears hanging out at Darling this afternoon and into the evening. Focused on high tide line above the mouth of the river (north side).
The 13 rung ladder segment at the KM73 gully is missing the bottommost rung, making it a big step up/down from the platform.
Forest route between Tsocowis and Darling has not been brushed yet this season.  Trail is visible but crowded by foliage.
Multiple missing ladder rungs in this section, 4 on one ladder making it very difficult to pass. Use caution especially going down!!
Was able to cross Klanawa river without the cable car at the mouth of the river at low tide.
Small black bear foraging in the intertidal zone between Darling and Michigan. We gave him lots of space and he watched us pass by before resuming foraging on the beach.
The trail was mostly dry, offering summer-like conditions. However, there were some mud bogs between the 65 km and 64 km marks.
